title: Moving to Idaho from Nebraska; fight with brother hiding in an outhouse
description: Move to Idaho a result of letters from Nebraska friends who'd moved out earlier. Fight in Nebraska with brother Dick, hiding in an outhouse.

title: Renting a boxcar; train ride with three families; father hit his head
description: Parents rented a boxcar to carry belongings out. Train ride with three families. Father lost his sense of balance and hit his head.

title: Arrival in Princeton; moving out 30 days later
description: Arrival in Princeton. Moved out 30 days later.

title: Walking to school; seeing a bear; shocking hay; Mother's day
description: Walking to school. Seeing a bear near gate. Lolah Tribble plowed with a foot burner and shocked hay with her mother. Mother's day, did things the hard way.

title: Going to Moscow the first time
description: Five years after arriving in Idaho, she went to Moscow for the first time to buy a winter coat.

title: Father's rules at dances; honeymoon in Spokane
description: Boys went to girls' home to court. Father's rules at dances: dance with everyone; don't leave the dance hall. Honeymoon in Spokane.

title: Hershiel worked in the woods
description: Hershiel worked in woods some after marriage.

title: Hershiel logging; cooking for men at logging camps; time away from parents
description: Stayed near Collins once when Hershiel logged. Cooked for men once at logging camp near home. Only times away from parents.

title: Got used to accidents in the woods
description: Didn't worry about accidents in the woods. You just got used to it.

title: Nicknames; Hershiel; Uncle Shorty
description: Nicknames. Hershiel. Uncle Shorty. Dick. Babe (her own).

title: Moonshining; moonshine from Onaway called day-glow red
description: Moonshining. One still just up Hatter Creek. Some moonshine from Onaway called day-glow red.

title: Trip to Princeton; Hershiel built school for their children; teachers who taught there
description: Half day trip to Princeton. Hershiel Tribble and others built a school for their children. Lolah Tribble describes teachers who taught there.

title: Scared of the first man teacher she had; teacher who went to dances with the kids
description: Lolah scared of the first man teacher she had when moved to Princeton. Next man teacher was boss in the classroom, but would go to dances with the kids.

title: Neighbors were her closest friends
description: Nearby neighbors were Lolah Tribble's closest friends. Don't communicate like that now.
